chapter.label: Epistle 38
chapter.title: Epistle 38: The Letter of Caldonius, Herculanus, and Others, on the Excommunication of Felicissimus with His People

paragraphs.text: The Letter of Caldonius, Herculanus, and Others, on the Excommunication of Felicissimus with His People.
paragraphs.html: The Letter of Caldonius, Herculanus, and Others, on the Excommunication of Felicissimus with His People.

paragraphs.text: Argument.— Caldonius, Herculanus, and Others Carry into Effect What the Preceding Letter Had Bidden Them.
paragraphs.html: Argument.— Caldonius, Herculanus, and Others Carry into Effect What the Preceding Letter Had Bidden Them.

paragraphs.text: Caldonius, with Herculanus and Victor, his colleagues, also with Rogatianus and Numidicus, presbyters. We have rejected Felicissimus and Augendus from communion; also Repostus from among the exiles, and Irene of the Blood-stained ones; and Paula the sempstress; which you ought to know from my subscription; also we have rejected Sophronius and Soliassus (budinarius), — himself also one of the exiles.
